# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Monday 7/13

**[Monday 7/13 session outlook] Bullish lean — Strong expansion / bull · composite +36 · confidence 48%**

**🔮 Next-session forecast:** The next U.S. regular session is Monday, July 13, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST on 7/13). The analysis below is oriented entirely toward that upcoming session.

*Data as of: 2026-07-13T06:33:13 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

With the market closed and the next regular session set to open Monday 7/13 at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST 7/13), this brief lays out our forecast for that session. The overall market-state score stands at +36 out of 100, placing conditions in "Strong expansion / bull" territory, with a confidence reading of 48% built from four measured components. Six inputs — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of the composite, so the score should be read as a partial-information signal rather than a complete picture.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Heading into Monday's open, the major benchmarks closed their prior session with the S&P 500 at 7,575.39 (+0.42% on the day), the Nasdaq-100 at 29,825.11 (+0.33%), the Dow at 52,637.01 (+0.29%), and the Russell 2000 lagging at 2,977.81 (-0.49%). The large-cap and tech-heavy benchmarks are carrying positive momentum into the new session, while small caps show relative softness.

**Market drivers.** Three of four tracked drivers are currently supportive of risk assets: volatility, index trend, and the yield curve all lean favorable. The lone headwind is the U.S. dollar, which continues to work against the constructive setup.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +36/100 — corresponding to "Strong expansion / bull" — carries a confidence level of 48% on four measured components. As noted above, Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of this calculation, a caveat worth keeping in mind when sizing conviction around Monday's open.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership is concentrated in Financials, Health Care, and Industrials, while Consumer Staples, Real Estate, and Energy are lagging. This rotation pattern points to risk-on positioning favoring cyclical and quality-growth exposure over defensives, even as Energy's laggard status stands in some tension with the sharp overnight move in crude discussed below.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.54%, the Dollar Index (DXY) is at 101.11 (+0.14% on the day), WTI crude has surged to 74.35 (+4.12%), and gold has pulled back to 4,063.60 (-0.99%). The combination of a firmer dollar, a sharp oil rally, and softer gold will be worth monitoring as cross-currents heading into Monday's session.

## Outlook

**Short term.** As the next U.S. regular session — Monday 7/13, opening 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/13 — approaches, the near-term outlook is constructive, consistent with the "Strong expansion / bull" market state and its 48% confidence reading. This bias would flip if the overall composite score were to cross below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term view is similarly constructive, anchored to the same "Strong expansion / bull" classification and 48% confidence level. Here too, a reversal of this stance would be triggered by the overall score crossing zero.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term stance remains constructive on the strength of the "Strong expansion / bull" reading and its 48% confidence. As with the shorter horizons, this outlook is contingent on the composite score staying above the zero threshold.

## Top News

Coverage feeding into Monday's setup includes: "Where The Selling Ran Deepest: 1 S&P 500 Stock At 52-Week Lows" (Trefis); a report that gold dropped 0.56% to $4,064 as an oil surge and 72% odds of a September Fed hike pressured bullion (Economy Middle East); "S&P 500 Stocks At 52-Week Highs: Friday's Full List" (Trefis); "Stock Market: Will S&P 500 Open Up or Down Today?" (Benzinga); a piece on AI demand surging as power limits cap supply and firms chase ROI (CHOSUNBIZ); and commentary from a former Intel CEO arguing that "Only Energy Constrains AI, Demand Is Infinite" (Seoul Economic Daily).
